h1: Owens Corning Duration vs CertainTeed Landmark: Side-by-Side Comparison (2026)
lead_summary: Both shingles are mid-tier architectural products from top-three manufacturers. Owens Corning Duration leads on the SureNail triple-layer nailing strip, a wider 600 percent nail zone, and the TruDefinition color contrast. CertainTeed Landmark leads on color count (24 vs 19) and the heavier base construction. Material cost is within a few dollars per square; installed pricing is nearly identical at $450 to $600 per square in most markets.
choose_a: You want the SureNail triple-layer nailing strip and 600 percent wider nailing zone.
You prefer Owens Corning's TruDefinition color contrast for dimensional curb appeal.
Your contractor is an Owens Corning Platinum Preferred installer for the longest workmanship warranty.
You shop at Lowe's and want easy distributor access.
You prioritize hurricane-zone wind fastening (the SureNail strip is the install confidence-builder).
choose_b: You want the wider color library (24 colors vs 19) with Heritage and Designer collections.
You prefer the heavier 230 lb/sq base construction for a thicker visual profile.
You want CertainTeed's StreakFighter algae warranty (25 years standard).
Your contractor is a CertainTeed SELECT ShingleMaster for the extended workmanship coverage.
Your local supply house carries CertainTeed at a better price than Lowe's-distributed Owens Corning.

comparison_table.a_ctx: SureNail triple-layer nailing strip, 600 percent wider zone
comparison_table.b_ctx: Needs CertainTeed starter and ridge for upgrade
comparison_table.winner_note: OC Duration stronger base rating; both reach 130 mph

comparison_table.a_ctx: UL 2218; Duration FLEX upgrades to Class 4
comparison_table.b_ctx: UL 2218; Landmark IR upgrades to Class 4

cost_delta.notes: Both shingles run $450 to $600 per square installed in most markets, which is essentially the same price. The brand-level material difference is within $5 per square, often invisible at the project level after labor and accessories. Contractor relationships and local distributor pricing matter more than the brand-level retail spread.

reddit_quotes_note: Use the Reddit quotes already captured by the main scraper. Discussion of OC Duration vs CertainTeed Landmark on Reddit tends to focus on contractor preference and installer certification more than spec differences.

faq_items.q: Is Owens Corning Duration better than CertainTeed Landmark?
faq_items.a: It depends on your priorities. Duration has the stronger nailing technology (SureNail) and base wind warranty (130 mph). Landmark has the wider color palette (24 vs 19) and slightly cheaper material cost. For most homeowners, contractor certification matters more than the shingle brand.
Both are roughly 230 pounds per square. The weight is essentially a tie. If you want significantly heavier, look at Landmark Pro (270 lbs/sq) or designer lines like Belmont or Grand Manor.

CertainTeed Landmark is about $5 per square cheaper at retail. On a 30-square home, that is $150 in material cost — generally invisible at the total project level. Both run $450 to $600 per square installed.

faq_items.q: What is the difference between SureNail and Quadrabond?
faq_items.a: SureNail is Owens Corning's triple-layer nailing strip that creates a 600 percent wider nailing zone, helping installers hit the right area consistently. Quadrabond is CertainTeed's four-part sealant system that improves shingle-to-shingle adhesion. Different approaches to the same goal: wind resistance through stronger fastening.

Both are lifetime limited material warranty with 25-year algae protection at the base product. The warranty length on paper is essentially identical. What matters more is the workmanship warranty tier offered by your contractor (OC Platinum Preferred or CertainTeed SELECT ShingleMaster).

Either works. For hurricane zones specifically, Duration's SureNail nailing system has a slight edge for installer-friendliness, but CertainTeed Landmark with proper six-nail upgrade reaches the same 130 mph rating. Florida insurers do not prefer one brand over the other.
